title: coprosma wollastonii var. novoguineensis
intro: Coprosma wollastonii var. novoguineensis is a variant of the Coprosma species, commonly known as the mirror bush or mirror plant, which is a type of flowering plant native to the tropical regions of New Guinea. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Coprosma wollastonii var. novoguineensis include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
